headline: Camp Out keeps the Camp Dream GA community connected beyond summer
subheadline: Camp Out extends the spirit of camp through local gatherings and special events that help campers, families, and volunteers stay close to one another throughout the year.
stepsHeadline: The program helps bridge the gap between summer sessions

steps.title: Reconnect the community
steps.description: Camp Out events give campers and volunteers familiar touchpoints after Summer Camp ends.

steps.title: Create shared moments
steps.description: Gatherings may include activity nights, social events, and community-centered programs that feel approachable and fun.

steps.title: Keep the mission visible
steps.description: Year-round events help families and supporters remember that Camp Dream GA is a continuing community, not only a once-a-year program.

sections.title: Social events
sections.description: Public descriptions of Camp Out point to familiar community activities such as movies, dances, bowling, and other shared events.

sections.title: A gentler entry point
sections.description: For some families, Camp Out can be a helpful way to stay close to the community even when overnight participation is not the immediate next step.

sections.title: Volunteer continuity
sections.description: It also gives volunteers and supporters a way to remain involved between summer sessions.

faq.question: Is Camp Out the same as Summer Camp?
faq.answer: No. Summer Camp is the main overnight program. Camp Out is the year-round community side of the ministry.

cta.heading: Use the calendar and stories pages to keep up with upcoming events and community news.
cta.body: Camp Out works best when families and supporters can easily see what is happening next.
